brief_document.title: Sync topology configuration and scheduling
brief_document.purpose: Deliver configurable sync topology (full-mesh, ring, custom pairs) and the sync scheduler that triggers periodic syncs based on the configured topology and interval. Any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds.
brief_document.task: Implement sync topology configuration: full-mesh (every node syncs with every other), ring (each node syncs with its successor), and custom (explicit sync pairs). Implement the sync scheduler as a beamr process that triggers periodic syncs based on topology and interval. The scheduler fires pull-syncs to the target nodes according to the topology map. Does not implement the sync protocol (DIST-001) or merge logic (DIST-002).

requirements.title: Topology configuration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL accept a sync topology on database creation: FullMesh, Ring, or Custom(pairs). THE SYSTEM SHALL derive the sync partner list from the topology. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT have a default topology — the caller must choose explicitly.
acceptance: SyncTopology::FullMesh with 4 nodes produces 6 sync pairs (all combinations)
SyncTopology::Ring with 4 nodes produces 4 sync pairs (each to its successor)
SyncTopology::Custom accepts an explicit list of (source, target) pairs
Creating a distributed database without a topology is an error

requirements.title: Sync scheduler
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a sync scheduler as a supervised beamr process. THE SYSTEM SHALL trigger pull-syncs to partner nodes at the configured interval according to the topology. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT block the shard actor's write path while syncing.
acceptance: The scheduler fires sync operations at the configured interval
Sync targets are determined by the topology
The scheduler is supervised — a crash restarts it
Write operations are not blocked during scheduled syncs

requirements.title: Convergence guarantee
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure that any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds. THE SYSTEM SHALL document the convergence properties of each topology: FullMesh converges in 1 round, Ring converges in N-1 rounds, Custom depends on the pair graph connectivity.
acceptance: FullMesh topology converges all nodes to the same state in one sync round
Ring topology converges in at most N-1 rounds for N nodes
Custom topology converges if and only if the pair graph is connected

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the sync protocol or node transfer — that is DIST-001
SHALL NOT implement merge or consistency modes — that is DIST-002
SHALL NOT implement dynamic topology changes at runtime — topology is set at creation
SHALL NOT implement node discovery — uses beamr's existing distribution connection manager
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/sync/ --include='*.rs' -l finds no non-test files
No file in crates/haematite/src/sync/ exceeds 500 lines

adrs.title: Content-addressed prolly tree over B+ tree with WAL overlay
adrs.decision: Content-addressed prolly tree over B+ tree with WAL overlay, because branching/merging/sync is the headline feature and must be structural (O(differences), history-independent) rather than overlaid (O(branch-writes), order-dependent). The rejected alternative (B+ tree + WAL overlay) makes branching an afterthought — functional but O(branch-writes) for merge and requiring WAL coordination for replication.
adrs.decided_by: Bono, Frodo Baggins, Sir Patick Stewart

checklist.text: Sync topology is configurable: full-mesh, ring, or custom sync pairs.
Sync scheduler triggers periodic syncs based on the configured topology and interval.
Any topology converges to consistency given sufficient sync rounds.

stories.text: As an operations engineer, I want to choose a sync topology (full-mesh, ring, custom) so that I can balance sync traffic against convergence speed.
As an operations engineer, I want to configure the sync interval so that I can trade freshness against network bandwidth.

constraints.text: Sync must transfer only content-addressed nodes that the target does not have. Full database copies on sync are a correctness bug.
Sync must operate per-shard. A sync on shard 5 must not block or affect shards 0-4 and 6-N.
Strong consistency mode must wait for quorum (N/2+1) acknowledgments before returning. Returning early is a durability violation.
Divergent writes must be resolved by the configured conflict resolution policy, never silently dropped.
Sync must use beamr's existing distribution connections. No separate TCP connections or custom transport.
A sync failure (network partition, node crash mid-sync) must not corrupt either node's local state. Partial syncs are safe to retry.

resolved_context.intention: Distribution extends haematite from a single-node storage engine to a multi-node system where databases on different machines stay in sync by exchanging content-addressed tree nodes. When this cluster is complete, two haematite nodes can sync a database by comparing root hashes and exchanging only the missing nodes — the same mechanism git uses for fetch and push, applied to a live key-value store. There is no primary-replica relationship, no replication log to manage, no WAL coordination. Any node can sync with any other node. The experience should feel like git remote: add a remote, push, pull, and the content-addressed structure guarantees that the minimal set of data moves.
